notes: This batch treats run_ultra4_full_stack.py as a standalone full-stack macro family.
ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep was read only as the next raw-order family boundary.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Catalogs ultra4_full_stack and ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep as adjacent ultra families.
Repo-held top-level evidence pack was searched for ultra4 and ultra sweep SIM_IDs and no matches were found.
Next raw-order narrower ultra sweep boundary.
Confirms the next family keeps Stage16 and Axis0 AB sweep structure while dropping geometry and Axis12.

runtime_expectations: run_ultra4_full_stack.py writes one results_ultra4_full_stack.json and one local evidence block for S_SIM_ULTRA4_FULL_STACK.
The runner uses 2048 Stage16 states, 128 Axis0 trials, 64 Axis0 cycles, and 4 stored sequences.
The adjacent ultra sweep runner uses a different contract: no berry flux, no axis12 branch, and explicit entangler/mix-mode metadata.
failure_modes: The ultra4 full-stack family can be mistaken for a repo-top evidenced surface because it has a local evidence writer and catalog admission.
The axis0_ab branch can be over-read as one uniform record contract even though it mixes absolute and delta records.
The adjacent ultra sweep family can be merged incorrectly into this batch because it shares the same macro strip.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
